pathophysiology_summary: A lateral ankle sprain is an inversion injury stretching the lateral ligament complex (most often the anterior talofibular ligament, ATFL). A Grade 1 sprain is a ligament stretch with intact joint stability, preserved weight-bearing, minimal swelling and no fracture. The ground-truth task is fracture exclusion using the Ottawa Ankle Rules — which are largely history/self-examination based and therefore feasible via telehealth: imaging is required only if there is bony tenderness at the posterior edge/tip of either malleolus, the base of the 5th metatarsal or the navicular, OR inability to weight-bear for four steps both immediately and at assessment. Here all Ottawa criteria are negative, so a fracture is clinically excluded, and the differentials (high/syndesmotic sprain, Achilles rupture) are excluded by mechanism and preserved plantarflexion. Management is early protected mobilisation (not immobilisation), RICE, analgesia and range-of-motion exercises.

differential.evidence_basis: Inversion injury, mild ATFL-region pain, able to weight-bear, minimal swelling.
Apply Ottawa Ankle Rules — bony tenderness or inability to weight-bear would mandate X-ray.
Inversion (not external rotation) mechanism and no AITFL tenderness argue against.
Anterior/lateral pain with intact plantarflexion and no 'pop' argue against.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Grade 1 sprain leads; fracture must be excluded with the Ottawa rules before reassurance.

differential.evidence_basis: Ottawa criteria all negative (weight-bearing 4 steps, no malleolar/5th-metatarsal/navicular bony tenderness), stable joint, neurovascularly intact — Grade 1 sprain; X-ray not required.
Ottawa-negative; escalation target if bony tenderness/inability to weight-bear emerges.

differential_progression.scoring_note: After a negative Ottawa screen, a Grade 1 sprain leads; conservative management, no imaging, with a fracture safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis; early protected mobilisation and physiotherapy; delayed imaging only if not improving.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Final position is a benign Grade 1 sprain.

red_flags.description: Ottawa-positive fracture features — bony tenderness at the posterior malleolar edge/tip, 5th-metatarsal base or navicular, OR inability to weight-bear 4 steps
red_flags.clinical_significance: Positive Ottawa criteria require X-ray to exclude fracture.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed malleolar/midfoot fracture mobilised as a sprain.

red_flags.description: Neurovascular compromise — numb, cold or pale foot
red_flags.clinical_significance: Suggests neurovascular injury/dislocation needing emergency care.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Ordering an X-ray for an Ottawa-negative sprain — unnecessary radiation and cost.
Advising rigid immobilisation for a stable Grade 1 sprain slows recovery; early protected mobilisation is preferred.
Reassuring without checking weight-bearing and bony tenderness could miss a fracture.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Clinician palpation of bony points and ligaments
Anterior drawer / talar tilt stability testing
Thompson test for Achilles integrity (though plantarflexion can be self-demonstrated)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Apply Ottawa rules via guided self-examination; if any bony tenderness or inability to weight-bear, arrange X-ray; otherwise conservative care.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Missing a fracture if Ottawa criteria not applied.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Ankle X-ray is indicated only if there is bony tenderness at the posterior edge/tip of either malleolus OR the base of the 5th metatarsal OR the navicular, OR inability to bear weight for four steps both immediately and in the assessment setting.
